text: Git branching is a powerful feature of version control that helps teams work on code without conflicts. A common workflow involves creating *feature branches*, which are copies of the main code where developers can safely add new functionality. Once work on a feature is complete, the developer submits a *pull request* to propose their changes. This allows other team members to review the code before *merging* it back into the main branch. Merging combines the changes from different branches into one version. This structured process keeps the main code stable and enables collaboration, making it easier to manage progress and undo mistakes if needed.
